zh.Dedham, Essex, England, United Kingdom: 英國英格蘭艾可迪德哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman於從1511年到1512年出生在英國。他於大約1540年與Agnes Perpoynt在英國艾可迪德哈姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1590年在英國艾可可爾斯特。

zh-hans.Dedham, Essex, England, United Kingdom: 英国英格兰艾可迪德哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman于从1511年到1512年出生在英国。他于大约1540年与Agnes Perpoynt在英国艾可迪德哈姆结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1590年在英国艾可可爾斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Sherman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): occupational name denoting someone who used shears to trim the surface of finished cloth and remove excessive nap, from Middle English sherman, shirman, sharman ‘shearman’.
Americanized form of North German Schürmann (see Schuermann ) and of German or Jewish (Ashkenazic) Schermann .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a tailor, from Yiddish sher ‘scissors’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Schuermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Franz, Guenter, Hans, Heiner, Klaus, Kurt, Udo.

North German (Schürmann): habitational name for someone from a place called Schüre(n), or a topographic name for someone who lived by a barn, from Middle Low German schüre ‘barn’. Compare Schurmann , Sherman , and Shurman .

variants.name: Shearer
meanings: Scottish and English: occupational name for a reaper of standing crops, a sheep shearer, or someone who used shears to trim the surface of finished cloth and remove excessive nap, from Middle English sherer(e) ‘shearer’, an agent derivative of Middle English schere(n) ‘to shear’. Middle English schere denoted shears and scissors of all sizes. Compare Sherman .
Americanized form of German or Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Scherer   or   Scherrer  , cognates of 1 above. Compare   Sharrer  .

variants.name: Shearman
meanings: English: variant of   Sherman  . The surname is also established in Ireland (Kilkenny).

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman was born from 1511 to 1512, in England. He married Agnes Perpoynt about 1540, in Dedham, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died about 1590, in Colchester, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman est né du 1511 au 1512, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Agnes Perpoynt environ 1540, à Dedham, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 1590, à Colchester, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman wurde von 1511 bis 1512 in England geboren. Er hat um 1540 in Dedham, Essex, England Agnes Perpoynt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1590 in Colchester, Essex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman è nato il da 1511 a 1512 nel England. Lui sposato Agnes Perpoynt circa 1540, nel Dedham, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1590, nel Colchester, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Shermanはから1511年まで1512年にイギリスで生まれた。約1540年にイギリスエセックスデドハムでAgnes Perpoyntと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1590年にイギリスエセックスコルチェスターで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman(은)는 1511년 부터 1512년 까지 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1540년 영국 에세스 데드함에서 Agnes Perpoynt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1590년 영국 에세스 코체스터에서 사망했다.

mn.Dedham, Essex, England, United Kingdom: Dedham, Ēastseaxe, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman nasceu de 1511 a 1512, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Agnes Perpoynt aproximadamente 1540, em Dedham, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1590, em Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman родился с 1511 по 1512 в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Perpoynt около 1540 г. в местности дедхам, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер около 1590 г. в местности колчестир, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Henry Sherman nació desde 1511 hasta 1512, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Agnes Perpoynt aproximadamente 1540, en Dedham, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1590, en Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra.
